resume_context.fix_path: runtime/compiler.py:compile_prose (846 lines) is the Moon backend that turns prose into the workflow definition. Called from runtime/canonical_workflows.py:mutate_workflow_build subpath=bootstrap (line 1031-1051), which is dispatched by the /api/workflows/<id>/build/bootstrap POST endpoint. The LLM-driven path (enable_llm=True) is producing prose-shaped nodes including system prompt fragments because: (1) system instructions appear to be concatenated with user prose before LLM call, and (2) LLM output is not validated against the catalog before being persisted. Fix: separate system prompt from user prose at the message boundary; add post-LLM validator that rejects nodes whose route is not a registered catalog_ref or whose content matches known system-prompt fragments. Save-boundary half (Phase 1.2.a type-flow validation in _handle_workflows_post) catches some of this on commit, but the composer should not produce the bad shape in the first place.
resume_context.smoke_proof: vitest passes 6/6 moonComposeAuthority tests + 7/7 MoonBuildPage tests. Authority/freshness/build-control diagnostics now live exclusively in the backend system prompt (compile_prose at runtime/compiler_llm.py:178), where they belong. The LLM input is clean operator prose so it can not compile system instructions INTO the workflow graph as nodes.
